Dominicans Don't Play.description: Dominicans Don't Play (DDP) is a Dominican-American street gang formed in Manhattan, New York, in 1991. Known for using machetes and knives, they are involved in drug trafficking, robbery, extortion, and murder. The gang operates primarily in New York City, New Jersey, and other U.S. states, as well as internationally. They have a large share of the cocaine market through association with Colombian cartels.

descriptions: Bloods are listed as rivals of DDP in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Bloods , Trinitarios , Latin Kings

descriptions: Bones is an offshoot of DDP.
edges.evidence_quote: 15-year-old Alex Ramirez, a member of the DDP offshoot Bones

descriptions: DDP has an association with the Colombian crime cartel for cocaine trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang holds a large share of the cocaine market due to an association with the Colombian crime cartel

descriptions: DDP is at peace with Latin Kings.
edges.evidence_quote: They also are at peace with the Latin Kings, a Puerto Rican-based gang who has had previous problems with their rivals the Trinitarios.

descriptions: Trinitarios are DDP's biggest rival since early 2000s, but were once united in the 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: The Trinitarios are DDP's biggest rival gang since the early 2000s, they were once united in the 1990s.

descriptions: DDP and Trinitarios were once united in the 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: they were once united in the 1990s

descriptions: DDP has an ongoing feud with sets affiliated with United Blood Nation.
edges.evidence_quote: They have had an ongoing feud with other area gangs, including the sets affiliated with the United Blood Nation

descriptions: MOD and MBS both splintered from Peace MOD, but no direct relationship between them is stated.
edges.evidence_quote: the breakup of "Peace MOD" caused it to break into three gangs, including the "Masters of Destruction", the "Mongolian Boys Society" (MBS) and the "Oriental Ruthless Boys" (ORB).

Hell's Lovers Motorcycle Club.description: Multi-ethnic outlaw motorcycle club founded in Chicago in 1967 by Frank 'Claim-Jumper' Rios. Active in various U.S. states with over 1,500 members. Known for drug trafficking and motorcycle theft, with a structured military-style organization. The club's motto is 'Death is my sidekick and the highway is my home.'

descriptions: A Marauders member allegedly killed a Hell's Lovers member, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Luther Vaughn Jr., a member of the St. Louis chapter of the Hell's Lovers, was shot and killed at the Jets Clubhouse Lounge, a nightclub in East St. Louis, on March 31, 1985, apparently by a member of the rival Marauders motorcycle club.

descriptions: Threats of violence between Hell's Lovers and Outlaws indicate conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: In June 2008, the Ironhorse Roundup Bike Show... was canceled... after state and Federal law enforcement authorities had warned... of threats of violence between the Hell's Lovers and a rival outlaw gang, the Outlaws.

descriptions: A Wheels of Soul member attacked Hell's Lovers clubhouse, and Hell's Lovers returned fire, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: On August 2, 2010, a member of the Wheels of Soul fired upon the Hell's Lovers' clubhouse in Denver with a shotgun from a sports utility vehicle. Three Hell's Lovers bikers who were inside the clubhouse returned fire.

descriptions: Hells Lovers Motorcycle Club is listed as an enemy of Wheels of Soul.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemies of the Wheels of Soul include the Outcast Motorcycle Club, Sin City Titans, Hells Lovers Motorcycle Club and the Street Soldiers.

descriptions: Wheels of Soul attacked the clubhouse of Hell's Lovers in 2010.
edges.evidence_quote: On August 2, 2010, Wheels of Soul biker Marshall 'Big Bo' Fry fired a shotgun at the clubhouse of the Hell's Lovers in Denver.

descriptions: MOD was in dispute with Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: the M.O.D was involved in a dispute with two other gangs; the Laotian Posse and the Alliance.

descriptions: Clan Spada contacted Franco Colò, a former Banda della Magliana member, to expand videopoker operations.
edges.evidence_quote: il gruppo degli "zingari" per allargare i propri traffici attraverso i videopoker si fossero messi in contatto con Franco Colò detto "il ciccione", storico braccio destro di Renatino De Pedis ai tempi della Banda

Los Rojos.description: Los Rojos is a faction of the Gulf Cartel, formed in the late 1990s as an armed wing providing security to cartel leaders. Based in Reynosa, Tamaulipas, Mexico, the group engaged in violent infighting with the rival Metros faction, losing power after 2012. It remains active in drug trafficking, murder, and kidnapping across several Mexican states.

descriptions: Los Rojos is a splinter group of Beltrán Leyva.
edges.evidence_quote: Fragmentación ... - Los Rojos , en Morelos y Guerrero .

descriptions: Guerreros Unidos initiated a dispute with Los Rojos.
edges.evidence_quote: Finalmente en 2011, El Tilde funda Guerreros Unidos e inicia una disputa con otros cárteles emergentes del extinto Cártel de los Beltrán Leyva conocidos como el Cartel del Pacífico Sur, La Mano con Ojos y Los Rojos, organizaciones que desaparecieron posteriormente.

descriptions: Los Rojos had a dispute with Guerreros Unidos, as stated by the governor.
edges.evidence_quote: El gobernador del estado, Graco Ramírez, señaló que el crimen había sido ejecutado por miembros del ya desaparecido cártel de 'Los Rojos', quienes sostenían una disputa con Guerreros Unidos.

descriptions: Guerreros Unidos splintered from Los Rojos.
edges.evidence_quote: It splintered off from another gang, los Rojos.

descriptions: Rojos is a faction within the Gulf Cartel, involved in internal conflict with the Metros faction.
edges.evidence_quote: The infighting between the Metros and the Rojos of the Gulf cartel began in 2010

descriptions: The Gulf Cartel created Los Rojos as a criminal group.
edges.evidence_quote: A lo largo de los años, el CDG creó grupos criminales como ... Los Rojos

descriptions: Cártel del Golfo created Los Rojos as a criminal group.
edges.evidence_quote: A lo largo de los años, el CDG creó grupos criminales como Los Ciclones , Los Metros , Los Escorpiones , Los Rojos , Los Sierra o División Sierra , Los Ceros, Fuerzas Especiales Grupo Sombra (F.E.G.S.), Los Zetas , entre otros.

descriptions: Los Metros and Los Rojos were in conflict from late 2011 to early 2012.
edges.evidence_quote: estos dos grupos estuvieron confrontándose desde finales del 2011 hasta principios del 2012

descriptions: Los Metros and Los Rojos are both splinter groups of the Gulf Cartel that agreed to a truce.
edges.evidence_quote: donde el Cártel del Golfo y sus escisiones como Los Metros, Los Ciclones, Los Escorpiones y Los Rojos afirmaban que comenzarían a una tregua

edges.evidence_quote: Their primary rivals in Morelos and Guerrero were Jalisco New Generation Cartel , the Beltrán-Leyva Cartel , and Guerreros Unidos .

descriptions: Los Rojos ordered the assassination of a Metros leader, triggering conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: On 2 September 2011, Mejía González and Rafael Cárdenas Vela , two leaders of the Rojos, ordered the assassination of the drug lord Samuel Flores Borrego , who commanded the Metros, another faction within the Gulf cartel.

descriptions: Rojos faction planned to ally with Los Zetas to end the war.
edges.evidence_quote: On 10 November 2014, a document from the Mexican government was released to the media and claimed that Los Rojos faction of the Gulf Cartel was planning to ally with Los Zetas.

descriptions: Los Rojos were suspected of being too soft on Los Zetas, and some members joined Los Zetas after the split.
edges.evidence_quote: Other sources indicate that the infighting could have been caused by the suspicions that the Rojos were 'too soft' on the Gulf cartel's bitter enemy, Los Zetas.

'Ndrina Giacobbe.description: The 'Ndrina Giacobbe is a 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Borgia, Calabria, Italy. It is allied with the Gaglianesi clan. After the death of boss Antonino Giacobbi, leadership passed to the Passafaro family, while the Pilò-Cossari family remains influential in the coastal area.

descriptions: The 'Ndrina Giacobbe is allied with the Cosca dei Gaglianesi.
edges.evidence_quote: La 'ndrina Giacobbi è una cosca malavitosa o 'ndrina della zona di Borgia alleata con la Cosca dei Gaglianesi in Calabria .

descriptions: The infobox lists Princ Gang as a rival of the Serb mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Princ Gang , UÇK , Naser Gang

Thuggee.description: Thuggee was a phenomenon of highway robbery in the Indian subcontinent, involving gangs that murdered and robbed travelers, often by strangling. It gained prominence in the early 19th century, when British colonial authorities portrayed it as a secret fraternity of ritual stranglers. Historians now view this portrayal as a colonial construct, with thugs likely being ordinary criminals or former mercenaries. The British campaign against thuggee in the 1830s led to thousands of convictions and influenced later legislation.

descriptions: Moltanee Thugs are a subgroup of the Thuggee phenomenon.
edges.evidence_quote: Notable groups include: - Moltanee (Multaneea) Thugs: deriving their name from the city of Multan.

descriptions: Sindouse Thugs are a subgroup of the Thuggee phenomenon.
edges.evidence_quote: Notable groups include: - Sindouse Thugs: based around the town of Sindouse on the Yamuna river...

descriptions: Telingana Thugs are a subgroup of the Thuggee phenomenon.
edges.evidence_quote: Notable groups include: - Telingana Thugs: from the Telangana region in the north of the Deccan.

descriptions: JCP competed against National Forces Alliance in elections.
edges.evidence_quote: it instead came a distant second to the National Forces Alliance

descriptions: Muslim Brotherhood formed the JCP in Libya.
edges.evidence_quote: the Brotherhood announced that it would form the Justice and Construction Party (JCP)

descriptions: The Laureana di Borrello locale in Sardinia is led by Ferrentino-Chindamo-Lamari-D'Agostino, which includes D'Agostino.
edges.evidence_quote: In Sardegna opera il locale di Laureana di Borrello , con a capo i Ferrentino-Chindamo-Lamari-D'Agostino.

descriptions: These two groups were opposed in the Faida di Laureana in the 1980s.
edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni ottanta quando nella faida di Laureana di Borrello aveva visto contrapposte le cosche Albanese-Cutellè-Tassone e i Ferrentino-Chindamo-Lamari-D'Agostino

descriptions: Bellocco supported the Ferrentino-Chindamo-Lamari-D'Agostino group in the faida.

descriptions: Harakat al-Nujaba is a PMF militia designated by US.
edges.evidence_quote: In March 2019, U.S. designated Harakat al-Nujaba and its leader Akram al-Ka'abi Specially Designated Global Terrorists (SDGT).

descriptions: Harakat al-Nujaba is a component of the PMF umbrella.
edges.evidence_quote: Approximately 800 members of Iraqi Shia militia groups, including Kata'ib Hezbollah, Harakat al-Nujaba, Sayyid al-Shuhada, and Badr Corps, had been sent to Iran.

Lopers 13.description: Mexican American street gang based in Santa Ana, California, founded in the late 1970s to 1980s. It is the largest gang in Santa Ana by territory, operating primarily in Orange County. The gang is involved in drug trafficking and other criminal activities, and has been targeted by law enforcement operations.

descriptions: Lopers 13 and Logan Street are rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Walnut street, Logan street

descriptions: Lopers 13 and Walnut Street are rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Walnut street, Logan street

Westmob.description: Westmob is a primarily African-American street gang based in San Francisco's Hunters Point projects, active since at least 1999. It is known for a long-running conflict with the rival Big Block gang, which was documented in the 2002 film Straight Outta Hunters Point. The gang is involved in drug trafficking and has been linked to rap culture. Members have been convicted for shootings and a high-profile murder of a police officer in 2004.

descriptions: Westmob has been in conflict with Big Block since 1999.
edges.evidence_quote: Since 1999, they have been in conflict with the Big Block gang, which is its major rival in the area.

descriptions: Westmob associates with Oakdale Mob.
edges.evidence_quote: Westmob is known to associate with the Oakdale Mob and Sunnydale Gangsters.

descriptions: Westmob associates with Sunnydale Gangsters.
edges.evidence_quote: Westmob is known to associate with the Oakdale Mob and Sunnydale Gangsters.

descriptions: Cardillo-Ranieri were scissionists who attacked Clan Mariano, leading to a feud.
edges.evidence_quote: L'origine della faida, secondo la ricostruzione dei Magistrati, risalirebbe al 24 marzo del 1991, Domenica delle Palme , in un agguato operato da Luca Mariani e da suo cugino Simone Festa, entrambi affiliati agli scissionisti Cardillo-Ranieri, nel tentativo di uccidere Giovanni Sole (allora considerato il braccio destro di Ciro Mariano), ma riuscirono a colpire a morte solo il suo autista, Ciro Napoletano.

descriptions: Riina distributed extortion money to Noce family.
edges.evidence_quote: e l’ultima parte alla Noce , ossia a Raffaele Ganci

descriptions: Wagner became part of the National Guard of Russia since 2023.
edges.evidence_quote: Part of: National Guard of Russia (since 2023)

Los Ninis.description: Los Ninis are the main armed wing of the Los Chapitos faction of the Sinaloa Cartel, based in Culiacán, Sinaloa, Mexico. Composed of young hitmen aged 20-35, they serve as security forces, torturers, and enforcers for Los Chapitos leaders. They are also tasked with monitoring drug labs, camps, and trafficking routes. The group is led by Néstor Isidro Pérez Salas (alias "El Nini") and is responsible for an internal war within the Sinaloa Cartel to impose Los Chapitos' dominance.

descriptions: Beltrán-Leyva Cartel is listed as a rival of Los Ninis in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Mexican government , Mexican Armed Forces , Los Rusos , Los Guanos , Beltrán-Leyva Cartel , La Plaza Cartel , U.S. government

descriptions: Gente Nueva is listed as an ally of Los Ninis in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Sinaloa Cartel , Los Chapitos , Los Chimales , Gente Nueva

descriptions: La Plaza Cartel is listed as a rival of Los Ninis in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Mexican government , Mexican Armed Forces , Los Rusos , Los Guanos , Beltrán-Leyva Cartel , La Plaza Cartel , U.S. government

descriptions: Los Ninis is the main armed wing of Los Chapitos.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Ninis are the main armed wing of the faction of the Sinaloa Cartel , known as Los Chapitos.

descriptions: Los Chimales is listed as an ally of Los Ninis in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Sinaloa Cartel , Los Chapitos , Los Chimales , Gente Nueva

descriptions: Los Ninis prevailed over Los Dámasos in conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: With him at the helm, they prevailed over " Los Dámasos ", a faction that sought to support Joaquín "El Chapo" Guzmán

descriptions: Los Ninis competes with Los Guanos for presence in Culiacán.
edges.evidence_quote: They also compete with members of " Los Guanos " faction, which also have a presence in the center of Culiacán .

descriptions: Los Ninis and Los Rusos dispute control of synthetic drugs and have clashed.
edges.evidence_quote: On November 25, 2019, members of the " Los Rusos " faction beat 11 municipal police officers who protected "Los Chapitos" for participating in extortion activities. Generating that both groups dispute the control of the production and sale of synthetic drugs in Culiacán

descriptions: Los Ninis is an armed wing of the Sinaloa Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Ninis are the main armed wing of the faction of the Sinaloa Cartel , known as Los Chapitos.

The Firm.description: English organized crime group based in Bethnal Green, London, led by the Kray twins from the late 1950s until their arrest in 1968. Involved in murder, armed robbery, arson, protection rackets, gambling, and assaults. Gained celebrity status in the 1960s before being convicted and sentenced to life imprisonment.

descriptions: The Cotroni crime family used the Krays to sell stolen bonds in Britain, a cooperative business deal.
edges.evidence_quote: The Montreal-based Cotroni crime family, the Canadian satellite of New York's Bonanno crime family, decided to sell the stolen bonds in Britain through the Krays.

descriptions: The two gangs were in a turf war, a conflict relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: The Richardson gang and the Kray twins were engaged in a turf war in the mid-to-late 1960s.

descriptions: Infobox lists Kray Firm as rivals of the Richardson Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Kray Firm

descriptions: The Kray twins formed an alliance with the American Mafia for money laundering and business ventures.
edges.evidence_quote: During the 1960s, the Kray twins formed an alliance with the Commission, the governing body of the American Mafia.

descriptions: The text mentions alleged links between the American Mafia and the Kray gang, but the nature is unspecified.
edges.evidence_quote: The evidence was weakened by Cooper, who claimed that he was an agent for the US Treasury Department investigating links between the American Mafia and the Kray gang.

descriptions: The Krays and Billy Hill's organization had protection arrangements.
edges.evidence_quote: Further contacts – and protection arrangements – were made between other American casino owners, the Krays, the Nashes and Pyle.

descriptions: The Krays and Foreman cooperated in a federation of gangs.
edges.evidence_quote: After the Richardsons were jailed in 1967, the Krays, the Nashes, Foreman and Pyle came to an informal agreement after a series of meetings about splitting the proceeds from their various protection rackets

descriptions: The Krays and Nashes cooperated in a federation of gangs.
edges.evidence_quote: After the Richardsons were jailed in 1967, the Krays, the Nashes, Foreman and Pyle came to an informal agreement after a series of meetings about splitting the proceeds from their various protection rackets

descriptions: The Richardson Gang were direct rivals to the Kray Twins.
edges.evidence_quote: South London was controlled at that time by the Richardson Gang, who were seen as direct rivals to the Kray Twins.

descriptions: The Richardson Gang is described as a rival of the Krays' Firm.
edges.evidence_quote: At Shepton Mallet they met Charlie Richardson, another inmate, who later formed the Richardson Gang, a rival of the Krays’ Firm.

descriptions: Ronnie Kray killed a member of the Richardson Gang, indicating conflict between the two gangs.
edges.evidence_quote: Ronnie shot and killed George Cornell, a member of the Richardson Gang, at the Blind Beggar pub in Whitechapel on 9 March 1966.

descriptions: Joey Pyle was associated with both groups, implying a cooperative relationship between them.
edges.evidence_quote: An associate of the Krays and the Richardsons

descriptions: Pyle mediated between the Krays and Richardsons, preventing conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Sometimes the Twins and the Richardsons would be at the same club at the same time but nothing ever sparked off while I was around.

descriptions: Edmonton Firm is an ally of TMD.
edges.evidence_quote: Anthony Bourne, member of TMD allies Edmonton Firm

descriptions: Infobox lists Edmonton Firm as ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Edmonton Firm (also known as The Firm, Edmonton Mandem, or EMD)

descriptions: George Cornell, a member of the Watney Streeters, became involved with the Krays.
edges.evidence_quote: Cornell was originally a member of an East End gang called "The Watney Streeters" and later became involved with the Krays.

descriptions: Los Huistas imposed taxes on Tío Balde, causing tensions.
edges.evidence_quote: En 2024-2025, surgieron tensiones cuando Los Huistas comenzaron a cobrar "impuestos" por el paso de drogas a otros grupos locales, como la banda de "Tío Balde".

descriptions: Tío Balde band joined with CJNG to form Cartel Chiapas-Guatemala.
edges.evidence_quote: Esto llevó a que estos grupos locales se aliaran con el CJNG para formar el Cartel Chiapas-Guatemala

descriptions: La Empresa was part of the Golden Triangle Alliance, a cooperative alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: La Empresa formed during this time and was one of the gangs.

descriptions: Hezbollah fought against the SLA during the 1980s and 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: Throughout the 1980s and 1990s, Hezbollah fought against Israeli forces and the South Lebanon Army (SLA)

descriptions: Hezbollah fought against the South Lebanon Army.
edges.evidence_quote: Throughout the 1980s and 1990s, Hezbollah fought against Israeli forces and the South Lebanon Army (SLA)

descriptions: Hezbollah fought against the SLA during the South Lebanon conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Throughout the 1980s and 1990s, Hezbollah fought against Israeli forces and the South Lebanon Army (SLA), eventually leading to Israel's withdrawal from southern Lebanon in 2000.

descriptions: Hezbollah fought against the South Lebanon Army during the 1980s and 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: Throughout the 1980s and 1990s, Hezbollah fought against Israeli forces and the South Lebanon Army (SLA), eventually leading to Israel's withdrawal from southern Lebanon in 2000.

edges.source: Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine
descriptions: PFLP members were killed in a clash with the SLA.
edges.evidence_quote: On 8 June 1990 a clash occurred near Shuwayya in South Lebanon in which four members of the PFLP were killed by the South Lebanon Army (SLA).

'Ndrina Costa.description: The Costa are a 'ndrina (organized crime clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Siderno, Italy. Initially allied with the Commisso clan, they later became rivals. They were involved in drug trafficking in Turin, Gorizia, and Modena. A war with the Commisso clan in the late 1980s and early 1990s led to the deaths of several Costa members. The clan is now largely defunct, with surviving members imprisoned or cooperating with authorities.

descriptions: Costa 'ndrina is listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Cataldo 'ndrina , Mazzaferro 'ndrina , Costa 'ndrina, Ierinò 'ndrina

descriptions: Curciarello initially allied with Costa, switched to Commisso during faida, then returned to Costa.
edges.evidence_quote: I Curciarello sono stati inizialmente alleati dei Costa, con lo scatenarsi della faida negli anni 1980 sono passati ai Commisso per poi successivamente ritornare con i Costa.

descriptions: Sia listed as ally of Costa in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Alleati - Sia

descriptions: Ursino listed as ally of Costa in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Alleati - Ursino

descriptions: Costa and Commisso were opposing sides in the Faida di Siderno.
edges.evidence_quote: Faida di Siderno : combattuta a partire dal 1987 quando la 'ndrina dei Costa volle rendersi autonoma dalla potente 'ndrina dei Commisso

descriptions: Costa became rival faction of Commisso.
edges.evidence_quote: Successivamente diventano fazione rivale.

descriptions: Costa initially allied with Commisso, later became rival faction.
edges.evidence_quote: I Costa sono una 'ndrina di Siderno , inizialmente alleati dei Commisso . Successivamente diventano fazione rivale.

descriptions: Ierinò are allies of Costa.
edges.evidence_quote: Sono alleati dei Mazzaferro dei Cataldo e dei Costa .

descriptions: Mazzaferro is allied with Costa.
edges.evidence_quote: Sono alleati con le 'ndrine degli Ierinò , dei Costa e dei Cataldo

descriptions: The text states 'Ndrina Sia is allied with Costa.
edges.evidence_quote: È alleata dei ... i Costa di Siderno

descriptions: Commisso and Costa 'ndrina feuded over control of Siderno.
edges.evidence_quote: In the 1980s, the Commisso were challenged by the Costa 'ndrina over the command of Siderno.

descriptions: Commisso was in a feud with Costa.
edges.evidence_quote: Alla fine degli anni ottanta scoppia la faida con la 'ndrina dei Costa

descriptions: Tehrik-e-Khilafat Pakistan merged into ISKP in January 2015.
edges.evidence_quote: These groups were Tehrik-e-Khilafat Khorasan (TKK), Khilafat Afghan and Muslim Dost's group, Azizullah Haqqani's group, and Tehrik-e-Khilafat Pakistan (TKP)

descriptions: TKP merged to form ISKP.
edges.evidence_quote: Though ISIS–K, when it was officially declared in January 2015, was the product of the merger of Tehrik-e Khilafat Khorasan (TKK), Khilafat Afghan, Muslim Dost's group, Azizullah Haqqani's group, and Tehrik-e Khilafat Pakistan (TKP)

Kadyrovites.description: Informal term for Chechnya-based detachments of Russian state agencies, effectively serving as Ramzan Kadyrov's private army. Formed in the late 1990s under Akhmad Kadyrov, they have been accused of human rights abuses including torture and extrajudicial killings. Active in Chechnya and involved in conflicts such as the Syrian Civil War and the Russian invasion of Ukraine.

descriptions: Azov Regiment fought against Chechen troops during the Siege of Mariupol.
edges.evidence_quote: The National Guard of Ukraine released a video appearing to show fighters from the Azov Regiment, based in Mariupol, greasing bullets in lard (salo) to be used against Chechen troops as an insult

descriptions: Chechen Death Battalion is a Chechen militant force loyal to Kadyrov, part of Kadyrovite forces in Ukraine.
edges.evidence_quote: Chechen militants loyal to Kadyrov have been active in the Russo-Ukrainian War since 2014. These forces include the Vostok Battalion and the Chechen Death Battalion.

descriptions: Kadyrovites were formed from pardoned former militants of the Chechen Republic of Ichkeria.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2015 BBC reported that a considerable part of Kadyrovites were Chechen Republic of Ichkeria militants pardoned by Russian president Vladimir Putin under the word of Ramzan Kadyrov

descriptions: Kadyrovites were part of the FSB from 2000 to 2004.
edges.evidence_quote: Part of: Federal Security Service (2000–2004)

descriptions: Goretz was a detachment within the Kadyrovites.
edges.evidence_quote: The Goretz (Mountaineer) detachment of the Kadyrovites was a spetsnaz unit of the FSB headed by Movladi Baisarov

descriptions: Kadyrovite troops executed conscripted soldiers of the Luhansk People's Republic.
edges.evidence_quote: Ukrainian media reported that Chechen troops executed conscripted soldiers of the Luhansk People's Republic who refused to fight.

descriptions: Special Battalion Zapad sided against Kadyrovites in a confrontation with Goretz.
edges.evidence_quote: The Kadyrovites ended up backing down in that confrontation when another Chechen warlord, Said-Magomed Kakiev, head of the Spetsnaz GRU unit the Special Battalion Zapad, came down on Baisarov's side.

descriptions: Vostok Battalion is a Chechen militant force loyal to Kadyrov, part of Kadyrovite forces in Ukraine.
edges.evidence_quote: Chechen militants loyal to Kadyrov have been active in the Russo-Ukrainian War since 2014. These forces include the Vostok Battalion and the Chechen Death Battalion.

descriptions: Former Wagner fighters joined Akhmat units.
edges.evidence_quote: Other reports indicate that former Wagner fighters have joined Chechen Akhmat units, whilst still wearing Wagner patches.

descriptions: Raïa Mutomboki was aligned with M23 and perpetrated violence in support.
edges.evidence_quote: Raïa Mutomboki, aligned with M23, perpetrated analogous acts of violence.

descriptions: Russian Ku Klux Klan is a group that imitates the Ku Klux Klan.
edges.evidence_quote: a group calling itself the Russian Ku Klux Klan staged a mock execution

descriptions: Burger Bar Boys listed as an ally in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Broadwater Farm Posse, Burger Bar Boys, Causeway Gang, Edmonton Firm (also known as The Firm, Edmonton Mandem, or EMD), Star Gang

descriptions: Infobox lists Burger Bar Boys as ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Burger Bar Boys

Egan's Rats.description: American organized crime gang based in St. Louis, Missouri, active from 1890 to 1924. Founded by Thomas 'Snake' Kinney and Thomas Egan, it engaged in bootlegging, labor slugging, voter intimidation, armed robbery, and murder. The gang was predominantly Irish-American and became the dominant criminal organization in St. Louis before being dismantled after a mail robbery conviction.

descriptions: The Bottoms Gang feuded with Egan's Rats.
edges.evidence_quote: They feuded with the larger Egan's Rats gang

descriptions: The Bottoms Gang was a rival of Egan's Rats, and they engaged in conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: The Egan Gang's main rival at this time was the violent Bottoms Gang from the Twenty-second Ward.

descriptions: The Cuckoo Gang provided a tip to Egan's Rats for a robbery, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: The Rats took control of the truck and made off with $2.4 million in cash and negotiable bonds after receiving a tip from the Cuckoo Gang.

descriptions: Egan's Rats fought a war with the Hogan Gang after suspecting them of murdering Willie Egan.
edges.evidence_quote: Egan's Rats soon went to war with the rival Hogan Gang, whom they suspected of killing Willie Egan.

descriptions: Egan's Rats and the Hogan Gang fought the Egan-Hogan War.
edges.evidence_quote: The gangs engaged in the Egan-Hogan War of 1921–23, which resulted in the breakup of Egan's Rats.

descriptions: The River Gang was formed by former Egan's Rats members, indicating a successor relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: Two other former members, Pete and Thomas 'Yonnie' Licavoli, started the 'River Gang', a large Detroit bootlegging gang.

descriptions: The Green Ones fought with Egan's Rats over bootlegging rackets.
edges.evidence_quote: Giannola attempted to take control of the bootlegging rackets in the area and fought with members of the Egan's Rats gang and Cuckoos Gang.

descriptions: The Hogan Gang and Egan's Rats were engaged in a violent gang war.
edges.evidence_quote: They fought a notoriously violent gang war with Egan's Rats in the early 1920s.

descriptions: Fred Burke, an associate of the Purple Gang, was originally connected to Egan's Rats.
edges.evidence_quote: Fred Burke, an associate of the Purples originally connected to the Egan's Rats gang of St. Louis

descriptions: Mancuso, Barbaro-Papalia, and Ursino-Macrì were targeted together in operation Tamburo, indicating they were jointly involved in drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: operazione "Tamburo" ... ha portato all'arresto ... presunte affiliate ai Mancuso, Barbaro - Papalia e agli Ursino - Macrì

descriptions: Members of Ursino-Macrì were arrested alongside Barbaro-Papalia affiliates in the same drug trafficking operation.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 14 gennaio 2014 si conclude a Milano , l'operazione “Tamburo”, iniziata nel 2009 che ha portato all'arresto per traffico di droga di 13 persone, presunte affiliate ai Mancuso , Barbaro - Papalia e agli Ursino- Macrì

descriptions: Westside Nation was a breakaway group from Bandidos, leading to a conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: In July 2013, a conflict erupted between the Bandidos and the Westside Nation, a breakaway group of former members of the club's 'Westside' chapter in Næstved who defected following a dispute over the club's national leadership.

descriptions: Some Westside Nation members joined Satudarah, forming its Copenhagen chapter.
edges.evidence_quote: The Westside Nation disbanded in 2014, with some members returning to the Bandidos and others patching over to Satudarah, forming the core of the club's Copenhagen chapter.

descriptions: Chief Squad is a splinter group of Baghdad Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: Other splinter groups include ChinaSquadNyalenda Base, Chief Squad, Nyamasaria Massive, Kenda Kenda, Kondele Baghdad for Peace, Karamojong Boys, Saba Saba, Artur Margaryan, Kebago, and American Marines.

descriptions: Raymond Avenue Crips is listed as an ally of Tongan Crip Gang in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Sons of Samoa , Tongan Foe Lyfe Ganster Crip, Raymond Avenue Crips
